title: Comeback scorecard
description: Post-render quality scoring borrowed from Codex Presentations skill Phase 5 (Comeback Rubric). 10 dimensions, each 0-5. Gates publish: by default the issue must total >= 40/45 (no reference supplied) or >= 44/50 (reference supplied), and no single dimension may score below 4. Profile gates and blocking anti-patterns sit ABOVE the numeric rubric. Lives at output/<slug>/qa/scorecard.json.

scored_by.description: Who or what produced this score (agent / human / hybrid). Free text.

story.description: Titles are claims; spread sequence has a real arc.

specificity.description: Deck would fail the noun-swap test. Replace subject and it stops working.

rhythm.description: Contact sheet has varied macro layouts; no 3 consecutive spreads share the same shape.

whitespace.description: Spreads breathe without feeling empty. Boxed prose has visible top/bottom padding.

image_clarity.description: Image-led equivalent of Presentations' chart_clarity. Each image proves one thing; crops are intentional; subjects are sharp; provenance verified for identity assets.

typography.description: Type feels intentional, not default. Fallback chain is used deliberately.

restraint.description: No filler boxes, badges, decorative clutter; no rounded cards as default.

precision.description: Metrics, dates, source notes, captions are exact. No invented facts.

coherence.description: One visual system across all 9 spreads. Margins / kicker / accent rule / page numbers consistent.

reference_delta.description: Visibly better than the supplied reference. Score 'n/a' or omit when no reference.

profile_gates.description: Pass/fail above the numeric rubric. Inherited from library/profiles/<name>.yaml.hard_gates.

anti_patterns_detected.description: Items from the blocking anti-patterns catalogue actually seen on this run. Severity 'block' fails the gate even if the numeric rubric passes.

where.description: Spread idx or region id where the pattern was observed.

iteration_targets.description: Spreads flagged for re-do before delivery. Empty when scorecard passes. Drives the iteration rule from Presentations Phase 5: 'iterate the weakest 2-4 spreads first, then rerender'.

narrative_summary.description: Blunt 2-3 sentence summary of where this issue lands. Names remaining weak spots; no vague praise.

evidence.description: Concrete observation justifying this score. No vague praise like 'good story'.

what_would_make_it_5.description: Optional. Concrete change required to reach 5. Required in practice when score < 5.
